content.problemStatement:
in Chefland, there is a very famous street where NNN types of street food (numbered 111 through NNN) are offered. For each valid iii, there are SiS_iSi​ stores that offer food of the iii-th type, the price of one piece of food of this type is ViV_iVi​ (the same in each of these stores) and each day, PiP_iPi​ people come to buy it; each of these people wants to buy one piece of food of the iii-th type.

Chef is planning to open a new store at this street, where he would offer food of one of these NNN types. Chef assumes that the people who want to buy the type of food he'd offer will split equally among all stores that offer it, and if this is impossible, i.e. the number of these people ppp is not divisible by the number of these stores sss, then only ⌊ps⌋\left\lfloor\frac{p}{s}\right\rfloor⌊sp​⌋ people will buy food from Chef.

Chef wants to maximise his daily profit. Help Chef choose which type of food to offer and find the maximum daily profit he can make.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains an integer N. N lines follow. For each i (1 <= i <= N), the i-th line contains three space-separated integers Si, Pi, and Vi.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum profit.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef should offer food of the second type. On each day, two people would buy from him, so his daily profit would be 121212.
Example case 2: Chef has no option other than to offer the only type of food, but he does not expect anyone to buy from him anyway, so his daily profit is 000.
Explanation: Chef has no option other than to offer the only type of food, but he does not expect anyone to buy from him anyway, so his daily profit is 000.

Explanation: Chef should offer food of the first type. On each day, one person would buy from him, so his daily profit would be 3 * 1 = 3. Chef should offer food of the second type. On each day, three people would buy from him, so his daily profit would be 5 * 3 = 15. The maximum profit is 15.

solution.approach: For each type of food, calculate the maximum daily profit by dividing the number of people who want to buy it by the number of stores that offer it, and then multiplying by the price of one piece of food. The maximum profit is the maximum of these values across all types of food.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * T) where N is the number of types of food and T is the number of test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) where N is the number of types of food.

code.Python3:
for _ in range(int(input())):
  N = int(input())
  maxProfit = 0
  for _ in range(N):
    S, P, V = map(int, input().split())
    maxProfit = max(maxProfit, (P + S - 1) // S * V)
  print(maxProfit)
